title: Add Containerfile to build a versioned stable image for quay.io
body:
I've been using this Containerfile to build versioned stable images
of Buildah for quay.io.  I build these images if I miss grabbing the
'latest' image before the next version goes to latest, or if we've
made changes to how the image is built and I need to redo the older
versions.

As we've had a few updates by different people to the Containerfiles
in these directories, I thought I'd add this one too so it can be
updated by others if needed.

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body: Tests are not happy.
Nit fixed, @nalind's comments addressed.  Time to merge?
bors r+

# Build failed
  * cirrus-ci/success
@cevich this was green before bors failed.  Is there something I can rekick?
bors retry

@TomSweeneyRedHat you should also have access to `bors retry` as dan did.

The answer to "why did it fail" is important too.  Click the red X above the comment the bot posted.
Hint: `curl: (6) Could not resolve host: download.opensuse.org` methinks the internet broke.

Thx cevich, I'll put that spell in my little magic spell box.  
I did not see a red X anywhere when I looked earlier, but perhaps it needed to be a RED # X #  for my tired eyeballs.

> for my tired eyeballs.

This is in the docs I wrote, so clearly you didn't read those :stuck_out_tongue_winking_eye: 

Seriously though, you're not the first one to miss it.  I think it's a poorly done github feature, but perhaps bors-ng could be made to drop a reference link?  Feel free to ask on the forum.

# Build failed
  * cirrus-ci/success

Seems that thing keeps failing over and over, `unable to resolve download.opensuse.org`.  I just tried it in my browser, and it really does appear to be missing/down:

https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/xUbuntu_19.04/Release.key

That's required for Ubuntu to function as part of testing.  Should we temporarily disable Ubuntu? :cold_sweat:
heh, in fact I can't even get to https://opensuse.org did they loose their domain?!?!?!?
Okay, merging this will stop the Ubuntu 19 madness: #2191
When I click the bors Red X, nothing happens?  What am I missing?
@saschagrunert @vrothberg What is going on with OpenSuse.org?

> @saschagrunert @vrothberg What is going on with OpenSuse.org?

Yeah, seems a bit down right now. :see_no_evil:

> When I click the bors Red X, nothing happens? What am I missing?

Oh?  For me it opens up a little pop-up with links and details for all the tests.  My bad for assuming browser-behavior was universal :disappointed: Hmmm, gosh, I poked around but I can't find a secondary way into that same data...this really is a terrible WebUI design.  I'll keep poking, there has to be a way...

...uggg, this is sooo bad.  @rhatdan I am not able to find a way in the github WebUI to access the trying or staging run by bors which doesn't also involve a popup.  Are you SURE you don't see one when you hit the red x in this line from bors bot: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2183#ref-commit-42a3728

Firefox: clicking on the eyeball brings me to bors info patch, details takes me to a useless list of tests and the Red X takes me no where

Google Chrome gives me the same.  I am on Fedora 32

Seems to be back up
bors retry

# Build succeeded
  * [cirrus-ci/success](https://github.com/containers/buildah/runs/475339019)

> the Red X takes me no where

Well! I'm on the FAR superior F30 :stuck_out_tongue_closed_eyes:  I shouldn't take you anywhere, it just brings up a pop-up like in the screenshot: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#interactionmonitoring
